message:
refactor(outcome): Convert required options to positional arguments

Change from:
  cm outcome --status success --rules b-123,b-456

To:
  cm outcome success b-123,b-456

This is cleaner UX since both status and rules are always required.

Closes: cass_memory_system-84gh

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-81t","title":"[Utils] daysAgo - Calculate past date","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:02.020032-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:20.343337-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:03.870255-05:00","comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-81t","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Function Signature
```typescript
function daysAgo(days: number): Date
```

## Implementation Details
- Create new Date() object
- Use setDate(getDate() - days) to subtract days
- Return Date object (not string)
- Handles month/year boundaries automatically

## Why This Matters
Used for filtering sessions by age (sessionLookbackDays config). Returns Date object for easy comparison with other dates. JavaScript Date handles edge cases like going back across month/year boundaries.

## Edge Cases to Handle
- Negative days - returns future date (allowed)
- Zero days - returns today
- Very large numbers - Date handles this
- Fractional days - Date.setDate handles fractional values
- DST transitions - handled by Date","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-81vv","title":"[CLI] Fix repo .cass path resolution in commands","description":"Several commands still treat repo-local files under .cass/ as relative to process.cwd(), which breaks when running from subdirectories (and can cause crashes when .cass/playbook.yaml is missing). Fix by resolving repo root via resolveRepoDir() and only using repo paths when the underlying file/dir exists.

Targets:
- src/commands/forget.ts: fall back to repo playbook using resolveRepoDir + fileExists
- src/orchestrator.ts: repo playbook path + hasRepo should use resolveRepoDir (git root), not cwd
- src/outcome.ts: resolveOutcomeLogPath/resolveContextLogPath/applyOutcomeFeedback repo paths should use resolveRepoDir and existence checks
- src/commands/playbook.ts: remove branch should not attempt to load repo playbook unless it exists; add branch currently has dead/untyped scope handling (clean up).

Acceptance:
- Running commands from a subdirectory behaves the same as from repo root
- Missing repo .cass/ does not crash; it cleanly falls back or reports not found
- bun test passes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-13T06:37:50.411262-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-13T06:44:52.338891-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-13T06:44:52.338891-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-81x","title":"[Curator] autoInvertHarmfulBullets - Batch inversion check","description":"Post-processing phase that automatically inverts bullets with consistently negative feedback.

Signature:
function autoInvertHarmfulBullets(
  playbook: Playbook,
  pruneThreshold: number
): InversionReport[]

Algorithm:
1. Initialize inversions array
2. For each active bullet in playbook:
   a. Skip if bullet.pinned === true
   b. Skip if bullet.maturity === 'deprecated'
   c. Skip if bullet.kind === 'anti_pattern'
   d. Calculate effectiveScore = helpfulCount - harmfulCount
   e. If effectiveScore < -pruneThreshold:
      - Create anti-pattern using invertToAntiPattern(bullet)
      - Add anti-pattern to playbook.bullets
      - Deprecate original bullet:
        * state: 'retired'
        * maturity: 'deprecated'
        * deprecationReason: 'auto-inverted due to consistent negative feedback'
        * replacedBy: anti-pattern ID
      - Create InversionReport:
        * originalId: bullet.id
        * originalContent: bullet.content
        * antiPatternId: antiPattern.id
        * antiPatternContent: antiPattern.content
      - Add to inversions array
3. Return inversions

When does auto-inversion trigger?
- Default pruneThreshold: 3
- Bullet needs net -3 score (e.g., 0 helpful, 3 harmful)
- Consistent pattern across sessions
- Not a single-session fluke

Why this matters:
- Converts mistakes into institutional knowledge
- Prevents deletion of learning opportunities
- Creates explicit anti-pattern section in playbook
- Maintains accountability and history

This is called AFTER all deltas are processed in curatePlaybook.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:05.550937-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T14:28:58.364449-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T14:28:58.364449-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-84gh","title":"Improve outcome command UX with positional arguments","description":"## Context

The `outcome` command records implicit feedback from session outcomes. Currently it requires multiple options:

```bash
cm outcome --status success --rules b-123,b-456 --session /path/to/session
```

Both `--status` and `--rules` are required, making them awkward as options.

## Current State

```typescript
program.command("outcome")
  .requiredOption("--status <status>", "Outcome status: success|failure|mixed")
  .requiredOption("--rules <ids>", "Comma-separated rule ids that were shown")
```

## Proposed Change

Convert to positional arguments:

```bash
cm outcome <status> <rule-ids> [--session /path/to/session]
```

Example:
```bash
cm outcome success b-123,b-456 --session /path/to/session
```

## Why This Is Low Priority

1. The `outcome` command is in the "Advanced/rare" category
2. Most agents use inline comments `[cass: helpful ...]` instead
3. The current API works, it's just slightly awkward
4. This would be a breaking change

## Implementation (if pursued)

```typescript
program.command("outcome")
  .description("Record implicit feedback from a session outcome")
  .argument("<status>", "Outcome status: success|failure|mixed")
  .argument("<rules>", "Comma-separated rule ids that were shown")
  .option("--session <path>", "Session path for provenance")
  // ... other options ...
```

## Backwards Compatibility

If implemented, should probably support both:
- New: `cm outcome success b-123,b-456`
- Old: `cm outcome --status success --rules b-123,b-456`

## Decision

This is optional and low priority. Only implement if:
1. We're doing a major version bump anyway
2. User feedback indicates confusion with current syntax

## Part of Epic

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-87l","title":"[Reflector] extractSearchAnchors - Generate SEO for agents","description":"function extractSearchAnchors(diary: DiaryExtraction): string[]

"SEO for agents" - terms optimized for future search:
- Combine key terms from all diary fields
- Include technical terms, error messages, file patterns
- Limit to 10-15 anchors

EXTRACTION STRATEGY:
1. From accomplishments: action verbs + tech terms
2. From challenges: error types + problem domains
3. From keyLearnings: concepts + patterns
4. From decisions: technology names + approaches

EXAMPLES:
- "React Testing Library"
- "async/await"
- "JWT authentication"
- "CORS configuration"
- "TypeScript generics"

FILTERING:
- Remove common words (the, a, and, etc.)
- Remove duplicates
- Prioritize technical terms
- Include version numbers if present

PURPOSE:
These help future context searches find this diary

ALGORITHM:
1. Extract noun phrases from all fields
2. Score by technical relevance
3. Sort by score
4. Take top 15

Used when saving diary to cass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.975745-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:17:10.474283-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:16:53.932452-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-8ift","title":"[Test] Unit: playbook.ts - CRUD and bullet management","description":"# Unit Tests for playbook.ts

## Functions to Test

### loadPlaybook(path)
Test Cases:
- Valid YAML file → returns Playbook object
- Empty file → returns default playbook
- Missing file → creates default playbook
- Invalid YAML → throws with helpful error
- Schema validation failure → throws with details
- File with deprecated patterns → loads correctly

### savePlaybook(playbook, path)
Test Cases:
- Saves valid playbook to file
- Creates parent directories if needed
- Overwrites existing file
- Uses atomic write (temp file + rename)
- Preserves YAML formatting

### addBullet(playbook, data, sourceSession)
Test Cases:
- Adds bullet with generated ID
- Sets createdAt/updatedAt timestamps
- Adds to sourceSessions array
- Extracts agent from sourceSession path
- Validates content length
- Returns updated playbook (immutable)

### findBullet(playbook, id)
Test Cases:
- Finds existing bullet by ID
- Returns undefined for missing ID
- Case-sensitive ID matching

### deprecateBullet(playbook, bulletId, reason)
Test Cases:
- Sets deprecated = true
- Sets deprecatedAt timestamp
- Sets deprecationReason
- Adds to deprecatedPatterns list
- Returns undefined for missing bullet

### getBulletsByCategory(playbook, category)
Test Cases:
- Returns bullets matching category
- Empty array for no matches
- Case-sensitive matching

### findSimilarBullet(playbook, content, threshold)
Test Cases:
- Finds exact match
- Finds similar content (Jaccard similarity)
- Returns null below threshold
- Handles empty playbook

### isDuplicateBullet(playbook, content)
Test Cases:
- True for exact duplicate
- True for very similar content
- False for unique content

### recordFeedbackEvent(playbook, bulletId, type, sessionPath, reason?)
Test Cases:
- Adds event to feedbackEvents array
- Updates helpfulCount/harmfulCount
- Handles harmful reason field
- Returns updated playbook

## Test Approach
- Use withTempDir for file operations
- Use createTestPlaybook for setup
- Verify file contents after writes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:23:40.892472-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:52:36.556364-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:52:36.556364-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-8ift","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-ai52","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:28:10.098944-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-dwew","title":"[Config] loadMergedPlaybook - Combine global + repo","description":"Function signature:
```typescript
async function loadMergedPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

Steps:
1. Load global playbook (~/.cass-memory/playbook.yaml)
2. Load repo playbook (./.cass/playbook.yaml) - may not exist
3. Merge bullets:
   - Same ID → repo wins
   - Different IDs → keep both (dedupe by content)
4. Load toxic logs from both levels
5. Filter bullets against combined toxic log
6. Return merged playbook

Edge cases:
- Repo playbook may not exist (use global only)
- Both playbooks may have same bullet ID (repo wins)
- Content-based deduplication to avoid semantic duplicates
- Toxic log filtering happens AFTER merge

Returns: Combined playbook with repo-level overrides applied","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:38.937664-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:26:00.898763-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:25:32.320932-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e21","title":"[Security] Sanitization application points documentation","description":"Document WHERE to apply the sanitize() function throughout the codebase.

Application points (in order of data flow):
1. After cassExport, BEFORE passing to LLM (in diary generation)
   - Raw session content from CASS is untrusted
   - Must sanitize before LLM sees it

2. Before writing DiaryEntry to disk
   - Prevent secrets from persisting in diary files
   - Apply sanitize() to entry.content

3. Before storing content in PlaybookBullet
   - Sanitize before creating bullet objects
   - Apply to bullet.content and bullet.evidence

4. In context output (should already be sanitized if stored sanitized)
   - Defense in depth: verify sanitization at output time
   - Shouldn't find anything if earlier stages worked

GOLDEN RULE: NEVER pass unsanitized session content to LLM or disk

Implementation checklist:
- [ ] Sanitize in diary generation flow
- [ ] Sanitize before DiaryEntry persistence
- [ ] Sanitize before PlaybookBullet creation
- [ ] Add verification at context output

Priority: 0 (Security Critical - P12)","notes":"Added sanitization application points under README Security/Secret Sanitization; communicated to agents. No sanitize.ts changes to avoid conflict with WhiteCat reservation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:49.167852-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:48:03.200679-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:45:48.046117-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e63w","title":"[Docs] Architecture documentation - How it works","description":"Document system architecture and how cass-memory works internally.

Topics to cover:
- Three-layer memory model with diagram (Diary → Playbook → Project)
- ACE pipeline: Generator → Reflector → Validator → Curator
- Data flow diagrams showing session → diary → playbook → project
- Schema explanations for each data structure
- Why deterministic curation matters (reproducibility, auditability)
- Confidence decay algorithm explanation
- Semantic classification system
- Evidence-based decision making

Include visual diagrams (ASCII art or mermaid.js) and concrete examples","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:49:30.515929-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T02:38:27.298678-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T02:38:27.298678-05:00"}
-{"id":"cass_memory_system-e6o7","title":"Refactor onboard command from flags to subcommands","description":"## Context

The `onboard` command currently uses ~12 mutually exclusive flags to represent different operations:

| Current Flag | Purpose |
|-------------|---------|
| --status | Check onboarding progress |
| --sample | Sample sessions for analysis |
| --read <path> | Export a session for agent to analyze |
| --prompt | Show extraction instructions |
| --guided | Full guided workflow |
| --mark-done <path> | Mark session as processed |
| --reset | Clear onboarding progress |
| --gaps | Show category gap analysis |
| --fill-gaps | Prioritize gap-filling in sample |
| --template | Rich output mode for --read |

This is inconsistent with `playbook` and `privacy` which use subcommands:
- `playbook list`, `playbook add`, `playbook remove`
- `privacy status`, `privacy enable`, `privacy disable`

## Problem

1. **Discoverability**: Running `cm onboard --help` shows a wall of flags. Hard to understand workflow.
2. **Consistency**: Breaks the pattern established by other commands
3. **Cognitive Load**: Agents must remember flag names vs being able to explore subcommands

## Proposed Structure

```
cm onboard status          # Check status and progress (was --status)
cm onboard gaps            # Show category gap analysis (was --gaps)
cm onboard sample          # Sample sessions for analysis (was --sample)
cm onboard read <path>     # Export session for analysis (was --read)
cm onboard prompt          # Show extraction instructions (was --prompt)
cm onboard guided          # Full guided workflow (was --guided)
cm onboard mark-done <path> # Mark session processed (was --mark-done)
cm onboard reset           # Clear progress (was --reset)
```

Modifiers become options on the relevant subcommand:
- `cm onboard sample --fill-gaps` (was --sample --fill-gaps)
- `cm onboard read <path> --template` (was --read <path> --template)

## Backwards Compatibility

The old flag syntax MUST continue to work with deprecation warnings:

```
$ cm onboard --status
⚠️ Deprecation: `--status` flag is deprecated. Use `cm onboard status` instead.
[... normal output ...]
```

Timeline:
- v1.x: Both syntaxes work, old syntax shows warning
- v2.0: Remove deprecated flag syntax

## Implementation Strategy

1. Keep existing `onboardCommand()` function as internal implementation
2. Create new subcommand handlers that call into existing logic
3. Add deprecation detection layer that translates old flags to new subcommands
4. Update cm.ts to define subcommand structure

## Files to Modify

- `src/cm.ts` - Add subcommand definitions (like playbook/privacy pattern)
- `src/commands/onboard.ts` - Refactor to export individual handlers
- `src/commands/quickstart.ts` - Update command examples

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `cm onboard --help` shows subcommand list (not flag wall)
- [ ] All subcommands work: status, gaps, sample, read, prompt, guided, mark-done, reset
- [ ] Old flag syntax works with deprecation warning
- [ ] JSON output unchanged (backwards compatible)
- [ ] `cm onboard sample --fill-gaps --limit 5` works as expected

## Effort Estimate

2-3 hours

## Risks & Mitigations

**Risk**: Users have scripts using old syntax
**Mitigation**: Deprecation warnings, not hard breaks. Document migration.

**Risk**: Complex deprecation layer adds bugs
**Mitigation**: Comprehensive test coverage for both syntaxes

## Part of Epic

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-e8x","title":"[Playbook] loadPlaybook - Load and validate playbook from YAML","description":"## Function Signature
```typescript
async function loadPlaybook(config: Config): Promise<Playbook>
```

## Purpose
Load and validate playbook from YAML file. Handles missing files, corruption, and schema validation with automatic recovery.

## Algorithm
1. Expand playbookPath from config
2. Check if file exists
3. If not exists: create empty playbook and return
4. Read file content
5. If empty: create new playbook and return
6. Parse YAML content
7. Normalize keys (snake_case → camelCase for compatibility)
8. Validate with PlaybookSchema.safeParse()
9. If invalid: backup to .backup.{timestamp} and create new
10. Return validated playbook

## Error Handling
- **ENOENT**: File doesn't exist → create empty
- **EACCES**: Permission denied → throw error
- **Parse errors**: YAML syntax errors → backup and recreate
- **Validation errors**: Schema mismatch → backup and recreate

## Backup Strategy
When corruption detected:
- Backup to: `{playbookPath}.backup.{timestamp}`
- Log warning with backup location
- Create fresh empty playbook
- Never lose user data

## Implementation Notes
- Use fs.promises for async file operations
- Use yaml.parse() for YAML parsing
- Normalize keys for backward compatibility
- Always validate against current schema version

## Dependencies
- fs/promises
- yaml library
- PlaybookSchema (Zod schema)
- createEmptyPlaybook()

## Related Functions
- createEmptyPlaybook() - creates new structure
- savePlaybook() - writes loaded playbook back","notes":"Existing playbook helper implementations already present; no code changes made. Released reservation; feel free to take over.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:45.760512-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T18:58:10.124214-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T18:57:52.629716-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-eefl","title":"[Critical] Type System Alignment - Multiple Files","description":"Type System Alignment - In Progress

## Current Status
- Typecheck shows 13 errors (down from 27+)
- Main conflict: External linter/sync process reverts schema changes

## Core Issue
The linter removes `helpfulEvents`/`harmfulEvents` arrays from PlaybookBulletSchema, but code (mark.ts, curate.ts, scoring.ts) requires them.

## Options
1. Keep arrays: Update schema (being reverted by linter)
2. Remove arrays: Refactor all code to use feedbackEvents.filter()

## Fixes Applied (may be reverted)
- types.ts: Added helpfulEvents/harmfulEvents arrays
- playbook.ts: loadPlaybook now accepts Config|string
- sanitize.ts: SanitizationConfig accepts string[] extraPatterns

## Remaining Errors
- config.ts: auditLog field missing in sanitization default
- types.ts: BulletMaturitySchema should be BulletMaturityEnum
- validate.ts: supportingEvidence/contradictingEvidence missing

## Next Steps

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-efd","title":"[Types] PlaybookBullet Schema Part 1 - Identity and Scope","description":"## Overview
Define identity and scope fields for PlaybookBullet schema. These fields establish bullet uniqueness and applicability hierarchy.

## Identity Field

### id: string
- Format: 'b-{timestamp36}-{random}'
- Example: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8'
- Generation: timestamp in base36 + 4-char random suffix
- Purpose: Globally unique, sortable identifier
- Immutable: Never changes after creation

## Scope Hierarchy (5 Levels)

### scope: enum
Type: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

Hierarchy (broadest to narrowest):

1. global - Universal rules (e.g., 'Write clear commit messages')
   - Applies: Always
   - scopeKey: Not used
   - workspace: Not used

2. workspace - Repository-specific rules
   - Applies: When in matching workspace directory
   - scopeKey: Not used (workspace field holds path)
   - workspace: '/path/to/project'
   - Example: 'Use AuthService from @/lib/auth in this project'

3. language - Language-specific rules
   - Applies: When working in files of that language
   - scopeKey: Language identifier (e.g., 'typescript', 'python', 'rust')
   - workspace: Optional (can be workspace + language)
   - Example: 'For TypeScript, prefer interfaces over types for objects'

4. framework - Framework/library-specific rules
   - Applies: When project uses that framework
   - scopeKey: Framework identifier (e.g., 'nextjs', 'react', 'django')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For Next.js, use app router not pages router'

5. task - Task-type specific rules
   - Applies: When performing that category of work
   - scopeKey: Task category (e.g., 'authentication', 'testing', 'api-design')
   - workspace: Optional
   - Example: 'For authentication tasks, validate JWT expiration'

## Scope Filtering Logic
When retrieving bullets for context:
1. Include all 'global' scope bullets
2. If in workspace, include matching 'workspace' bullets
3. If file language detected, include matching 'language' bullets
4. If framework detected, include matching 'framework' bullets
5. If task category inferred, include matching 'task' bullets

Multiple scopes can apply simultaneously (e.g., workspace + language + task).

## Fields

### Required
- id: string - Unique identifier
- scope: 'global' | 'workspace' | 'language' | 'framework' | 'task'

### Optional
- scopeKey: string | undefined
  - Used for: language, framework, task scopes
  - Not used for: global, workspace scopes
  - Examples: 'typescript', 'react', 'testing'

- workspace: string | undefined
  - Used for: workspace scope (required)
  - Optional for: other scopes (adds workspace constraint)
  - Value: Absolute path to workspace root
  - Example: '/Users/dev/projects/my-app'

## Examples

Global Rule: { id: 'b-l7k9m2p4-a3f8', scope: 'global', content: 'Write descriptive commit messages' }

Workspace Rule: { id: 'b-m8n3p5q7-b4g9', scope: 'workspace', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/auth-service', content: 'Use TokenValidator from @/lib/auth for all token checks' }

Language Rule: { id: 'b-n9o4q6r8-c5h0', scope: 'language', scopeKey: 'typescript', content: 'Prefer const assertions over enums for string literals' }

Framework Rule: { id: 'b-o0p5r7s9-d6i1', scope: 'framework', scopeKey: 'nextjs', workspace: '/Users/dev/projects/web-app', content: 'Use server actions in app directory, not API routes' }

Task Rule: { id: 'b-p1q6s8t0-e7j2', scope: 'task', scopeKey: 'testing', content: 'Use vitest for unit tests, playwright for e2e' }

## Acceptance Criteria
- id field with format specification
- scope enum with 5 values
- scopeKey optional field
- workspace optional field
- ID generation utility function
- Scope filtering logic implemented
- Examples for all 5 scope types
- JSDoc explaining hierarchy","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:44:58.006278-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:18:30.004682-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:18:29.040192-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-y9k","title":"[Foundation] tsconfig.json with strict TypeScript settings","description":"Create tsconfig.json with strict TypeScript configuration for type safety and ESM module support.

## Context
TypeScript configuration is critical for ensuring type safety, proper module resolution, and IDE support throughout the CASS Memory System codebase. The configuration must support ESM, strict type checking, and clean import paths.

## Technical Specification

### Compiler Options:
- target: ESNext - Use latest JavaScript features
- module: ESNext - ES module output
- moduleResolution: bundler - Modern resolution for Bun
- strict: true - Enable all strict type checking options
- esModuleInterop: true - Better compatibility with CommonJS modules
- skipLibCheck: true - Skip type checking of declaration files for faster builds
- resolveJsonModule: true - Allow importing JSON files
- allowSyntheticDefaultImports: true - Allow default imports from modules with no default export
- forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true - Ensure consistent file name casing
- noImplicitAny: true - Disallow implicit any types
- strictNullChecks: true - Strict null checking
- strictFunctionTypes: true - Strict function type checking
- noUnusedLocals: true - Report errors on unused local variables
- noUnusedParameters: true - Report errors on unused parameters
- noImplicitReturns: true - Report error when not all code paths return a value
- noFallthroughCasesInSwitch: true - Report errors for fallthrough cases in switch

### Path Mapping:
- paths: { @/*: [./src/*] } - Clean imports like import { Playbook } from '@/types'

### Include/Exclude:
- include: [src/**/*] - Include all source files
- exclude: [node_modules, dist] - Exclude dependencies and build output

### Output:
- outDir: ./dist - Output directory for compiled files
- declaration: true - Generate .d.ts files for library use
- declarationMap: true - Generate sourcemaps for declarations
- sourceMap: true - Generate sourcemaps for debugging

## Reasoning

### Why Strict Mode:
1. Catch Bugs Early: Strict null checks prevent null/undefined errors at runtime
2. Better Refactoring: Type errors guide you when making changes
3. IDE Support: Better autocomplete and IntelliSense
4. LLM Integration: Type safety is crucial when parsing LLM responses

### Why ESNext Target:
1. Bun Support: Bun natively supports modern JavaScript
2. Smaller Polyfills: No need to transpile to ES5
3. Modern Features: Use async/await, optional chaining, etc.

### Why Bundler Module Resolution:
1. Modern Standard: Aligns with modern bundlers and runtimes
2. Better Imports: Supports package.json exports field
3. Bun Compatibility: Matches Bun's module resolution algorithm

### Why Path Mapping:
1. Clean Imports: @/types instead of ../../../types
2. Refactor-Friendly: Moving files doesn't break imports
3. Readability: Clear namespace separation

## Acceptance Criteria

- tsconfig.json exists in project root
- strict: true is enabled
- target: ESNext is set
- module: ESNext is set
- moduleResolution: bundler is set
- Path mapping configured for @/* -> ./src/*
- include/exclude properly configured
- outDir set to ./dist
- bun run typecheck runs without errors on initial project
- IDE shows proper IntelliSense for imports
- No implicit any warnings in editor

## How This Serves the Universal Agent Memory System

TypeScript configuration is the quality gate for the entire codebase:

1. Type Safety: Prevents runtime errors when parsing LLM responses, file operations, and config management
2. Developer Experience: IntelliSense and autocomplete make development faster and more reliable
3. Maintainability: Strong types serve as living documentation for the codebase
4. Refactoring Confidence: Type errors guide safe refactoring as the system evolves
5. LLM Output Validation: Zod schemas combined with TypeScript types ensure structured outputs match expectations

Without strict TypeScript, the system would be prone to runtime errors, especially when dealing with dynamic LLM responses and user configurations.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:45:44.254164-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:19:20.048682-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:19:15.791505-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ydz","title":"[LLM] generateSearchQueries - Task-based query generation","description":"Function signature:
async function generateSearchQueries(task: string, config: Config): Promise<string[]>

Schema:
{
  queries: z.array(z.string()).max(5).describe('3-5 diverse search queries')
}

Prompt:
'Given this task: {task}

Generate 3-5 diverse search queries to find relevant information in cass:
- Similar problems encountered before
- Related frameworks or tools
- Relevant patterns or best practices
- Error messages or debugging approaches

Make queries specific enough to be useful but broad enough to match variations.'

Implementation:
1. Build prompt with task
2. Call generateObject with:
   - schema: queries array
   - temperature: 0.5
3. Return queries array

Used by: Generator phase to expand task into multiple cass search angles.

Example: Task 'add authentication' → queries: ['JWT implementation', 'session management', 'password hashing', 'OAuth setup']","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T16:46:02.92747-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:16.260667-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T20:11:02.188879-05:00"}

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","title":"[Critical] Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes","description":"# Implicit Feedback - Auto-Mark from Session Outcomes

## The Problem
Current plan requires manual `cm mark helpful/harmful <rule>` commands.
Users won't do this. They'll use the tool, benefit from rules, but never give feedback.
Without feedback, the confidence system is useless.

## The Solution
Infer feedback automatically from observable signals:

### Signal Sources:
1. **Session Success/Failure**: Did the task complete? Errors? Retries?
2. **Time to Completion**: Fast = rules helped. Slow = rules didn't help or were wrong.
3. **Pattern Matching**: Did the session use patterns matching rules we showed?
4. **Explicit Signals**: User said "that worked!" or "that was wrong"

### Implementation:
```typescript
interface SessionOutcome {
  sessionPath: string;
  startTime: Date;
  endTime: Date;
  rulesShown: string[];  // Rule IDs shown in context
  signals: {
    completedSuccessfully: boolean;  // No error exit
    hadRetries: boolean;  // Multiple attempts at same thing
    userSentiment: "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" | null;
    taskDuration: number;  // Seconds
    errorCount: number;
  };
}

async function inferFeedback(outcome: SessionOutcome): Promise<FeedbackEvent[]> {
  const feedback: FeedbackEvent[] = [];
  
  for (const ruleId of outcome.rulesShown) {
    let implicit: FeedbackStrength | null = null;
    
    // Strong positive: Quick success with no errors
    if (outcome.signals.completedSuccessfully && 
        !outcome.signals.hadRetries &&
        outcome.signals.taskDuration < FAST_THRESHOLD) {
      implicit = "helpful";
    }
    
    // Strong negative: Errors or retries on tasks using this rule
    if (outcome.signals.errorCount > 2 || outcome.signals.hadRetries) {
      implicit = "not_helpful";
    }
    
    // Explicit overrides implicit
    if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "positive") {
      implicit = "very_helpful";
    } else if (outcome.signals.userSentiment === "negative") {
      implicit = "harmful";
    }
    
    if (implicit) {
      feedback.push({
        bulletId: ruleId,
        strength: implicit,
        source: "implicit",
        sessionId: outcome.sessionPath,
        timestamp: new Date()
      });
    }
  }
  
  return feedback;
}
```

### Sentiment Detection (Lightweight):
```typescript
const POSITIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that worked/i, /perfect/i, /thanks/i, /great/i,
  /exactly what I needed/i, /solved it/i
];

const NEGATIVE_PATTERNS = [
  /that('s| is) wrong/i, /doesn't work/i, /broke/i,
  /not what I wanted/i, /try again/i, /undo/i
];

function detectSentiment(sessionText: string): "positive" | "negative" | "neutral" {
  const positiveCount = POSITIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  const negativeCount = NEGATIVE_PATTERNS.filter(p => p.test(sessionText)).length;
  
  if (positiveCount > negativeCount) return "positive";
  if (negativeCount > positiveCount) return "negative";
  return "neutral";
}
```

### Feedback Weighting:
```typescript
const FEEDBACK_WEIGHTS = {
  // Explicit feedback (user ran command)
  "explicit_helpful": 2.0,
  "explicit_harmful": -3.0,
  
  // Implicit from session outcome
  "implicit_helpful": 0.5,
  "implicit_not_helpful": -0.3,
  
  // Implicit from sentiment
  "sentiment_positive": 0.3,
  "sentiment_negative": -0.5
};
```

### Post-Session Hook:
```typescript
// Runs automatically after cass detects session end
async function onSessionEnd(sessionPath: string): Promise<void> {
  // 1. Get rules that were shown for this session
  const contextLog = await loadContextLog(sessionPath);
  if (!contextLog?.rulesShown) return;  // Context wasn't used
  
  // 2. Analyze session outcome
  const outcome = await analyzeSession(sessionPath);
  outcome.rulesShown = contextLog.rulesShown;
  
  // 3. Infer and record feedback
  const feedback = await inferFeedback(outcome);
  for (const f of feedback) {
    await recordFeedbackEvent(f);
  }
  
  // 4. Update confidence scores
  await updateConfidenceScores(feedback);
}
```

## Why This Is Critical
- Manual feedback has ~0% adoption rate
- Without feedback, confidence scores never update
- System can't learn what works vs what doesn't
- Implicit feedback is "good enough" for most cases
- Can always add explicit feedback later for edge cases

## Trade-offs
- False positives/negatives possible (mitigated by low weights)
- Privacy: Analyzing session content (already in cass, so OK)
- Complexity: Need to track which rules were shown","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-07T17:08:16.860446-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:48:17.022665-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:48:09.838663-05:00","comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Problem: Users Won't Provide Manual Feedback
Nobody will consistently run `cass-memory mark b-xyz --helpful` for every useful bullet.

## Solution: Implicit Feedback Signals
1. Session outcome correlation (bullet shown + success = implicit helpful)
2. Audit-based feedback (violations auto-mark harmful)
3. Agent integration (batch marking)
4. Interactive batch marking mode

## Acceptance Criteria
- Track which bullets shown in each context call
- Correlate with session outcomes
- Implement implicit +0.5 helpful for success correlation
- Add --batch-helpful and --interactive flags
","created_at":"2025-12-07T23:15:50Z"},{"id":39,"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-ygng","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Problem: Users Won't Provide Manual Feedback

 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yj1z","title":"[Test Infrastructure] Test fixtures and factory functions","description":"## Test Fixtures and Factory Functions

Create reusable test fixtures and factory functions for deterministic testing WITHOUT mocks.

### Required Factories:
1. `createTestPlaybook(overrides?)` - Returns valid Playbook with controllable defaults
2. `createTestBullet(overrides?)` - Returns valid PlaybookBullet with all fields
3. `createTestDiary(overrides?)` - Returns valid DiaryEntry
4. `createTestConfig(overrides?)` - Returns valid Config with test-safe defaults
5. `createTestFeedbackEvent(type, overrides?)` - Returns helpful/harmful events

### Design Principles:
- NO mocks - use real data structures
- Deterministic - same inputs always produce same outputs
- Composable - factories can be nested/combined
- Type-safe - full TypeScript inference

### Example Usage:
```typescript
const bullet = createTestBullet({
  helpfulCount: 5,
  harmfulCount: 1,
  maturity: 'proven'
});
```

### File Location: test/fixtures/factories.ts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:23:03.081639-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-07T21:13:03.932345-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-07T21:12:32.212766-05:00"}
 {"id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","title":"[E2E Tests] Full ACE pipeline - session to playbook","description":"## E2E Test: Full ACE Pipeline - Session to Playbook

### Test Scenario
Complete end-to-end test of the Autonomous Curation Engine:
Session ingestion → Reflection → Validation → Playbook update

### Test Steps

#### 1. Setup
- Create temp directory structure
- Initialize cass-memory config
- Create sample session transcript file
- Create initial empty playbook

#### 2. Execute Full Pipeline
- Run `cm context` with session path
- Trigger reflection (automatic or manual)
- Verify diary entry created
- Verify playbook updated

#### 3. Validate Outputs
- Diary entry has valid structure
- Playbook has new bullets
- Bullets have confidence scores
- Timestamps are correct

#### 4. Verify Logging
- Each pipeline stage logs entry/exit
- Timing information captured
- Error paths logged appropriately
- Verbose mode shows full details

### Test Data
- Sample session with 20+ messages
- Mix of code, discussion, errors
- Clear lessons to extract

### Success Criteria
- Pipeline completes without error
- Diary entry matches session content
- Playbook bullets are relevant
- All intermediate files created

### Verbose Logging Requirements
- Log: 'Starting ACE pipeline for session: <path>'
- Log: 'Reflection phase: extracting lessons...'
- Log: 'Validation phase: checking bullet quality...'
- Log: 'Curation phase: updating playbook...'
- Log: 'Pipeline complete. Added N bullets, duration: Xms'","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:24:28.156899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-08T01:36:53.861555-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-08T01:36:53.861555-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-yj1z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:24.284276-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-ep5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:24.445611-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-p4mv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:24.570764-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-33w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.148468-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-0thy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.273785-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-z8y7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.404948-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"cass_memory_system-yo24","depends_on_id":"cass_memory_system-70sd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-07T20:31:29.525025-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
